summary.intent: Investigate and fix GitHub issue #684 where the moltis web UI scrambles the ordering of sections in moltis.toml when saving settings.
summary.outcome: Root cause identified as toml_edit doc_position metadata being copied during clone() when inserting new sub-tables; fixed by stripping positions on insert, with regression tests added and PR created.

repo: Config save path: save_config_to_path() serializes with toml::to_string_pretty then merges into the existing document via merge_toml_preserving_comments()
There is a separate save_raw_config() path (used by provider-setup) that bypasses the merge entirely and writes directly
The web UI settings save goes through tools_routes.rs in crates/httpd/src/tools_routes.rs
Config schema uses skip_serializing_if attributes which can cause sections to be omitted from serialized output, potentially dropping sections during merge
227 tests in the config crate (226 after test cleanup)

code.finding: merge_toml_tables was doing updated_item.clone() which copies doc_position metadata from the serialized document, causing toml_edit to scatter new sub-tables to wrong positions. Fixed by stripping positions via clone_item_without_positions().

Added clone_item_without_positions() and clone_table_without_positions() helpers that deep-clone items but create fresh Table objects with doc_position = None, letting toml_edit auto-position them correctly.
Web UI config save endpoint calls save_raw_config via this route handler
Uses save_raw_config which bypasses merge logic entirely

workflow: toml_edit preserves document structure via doc_position metadata on Table items; cloning items between documents copies these positions and causes rendering artifacts
When doc_position is None on a toml_edit Table, the library auto-positions the table after its parent section — the correct behavior for new insertions
Key.decor() and Key.decor_mut() are deprecated in the toml_edit version used; use leaf_decor()/leaf_decor_mut() instead
CUDA build failures can cause clippy to fail on unrelated crates; run clippy scoped to the target crate to isolate issues

friction: toml_edit position metadata behavior is non-obvious and not well-documented; required writing multiple exploratory tests to diagnose
CUDA toolkit absence causes clippy failures that obscure real lint issues when running workspace-wide
Key API deprecations (decor/decor_mut) required a follow-up fix after clippy on the specific crate
open_items: save_raw_config() in provider-setup bypasses merge entirely — if it ever saves partial configs it could also scramble ordering, but was not investigated
Sections with all-None fields are dropped by toml::to_string_pretty due to skip_serializing_if — this could cause those sections to be removed during merge roundtrip; not fully characterized
No test coverage for the save_raw_config code path with respect to ordering
